name: Varsity Cinema
notes: Des Moines Film Society (nonprofit); est. 1938, reopened 2022 after restoration. Same Filmbot WordPress REST route as Gateway (/wp-json/nj/v1/showtime/listings). Coords approx — verify.

name: FilmScene
notes: Iowa City's nonprofit cinema (501c3); two downtown buildings — The Chauncey (3 screens) and The Ped Mall (1) — plus summer "FilmScene in the Park" outdoor screenings, all folded into one venue here. Sells via Agile Ticketing, but its own WordPress site (film-api-cron plugin) server-renders a clean day-grouped schedule at /now-playing/, so we read that instead of the Incapsula-fronted Agile backend. Dates carry no year (adapter rolls forward); film years come from /film/{slug}/ pages, only when explicitly labelled. Coords approx — verify.

name: Music Box Theatre
notes: Independent, operating since 1929. Sucuri Cloudproxy JS challenge fronts the whole site (probed 2026-07-05: no JSON/XHR reachable without the challenge cookie; /rss.xml bypasses it but is empty). Drupal underneath. ADAPTER (2026-07-06): Playwright headless with honest bot UA passes the challenge; parse the /calendar month grid (#dayN cells, .programming- content blocks, .times links; format in .film-format e.g. DCP/35mm). Calendar shows NO film year — TMDb matching is title-only for this venue. Film detail pages (/films-and-events/{slug}) do show year/director; a future improvement could fetch those for year disambiguation. Coords are approximate — confirm before relying on the distance filter.

name: Gene Siskel Film Center
notes: Nonprofit, public program of the School of the Art Institute of Chicago; Agile ticketing, but showtimes are server-rendered Drupal Calendar View — one month at a time (grid overflow covers the first days of the next month; the cron keeps coverage rolling). Detail pages give "year, director, country, mins". Coords approx — verify.

name: Kan-Kan Cinema & Bar
notes: Operated by Indianapolis Film Project, Inc. (501c3). WordPress site that SERVER-RENDERS listings — requests + BeautifulSoup works, no headless needed. Events at /events/{slug}/. Checkout hands off to Elevent (goelevent.com/Kan-KanCinemaandBrasserie). EASY archetype; Elevent backs other Midwest venues so this adapter should amortize — check for an Elevent JSON search endpoint before hard-parsing HTML.

name: Cinema Center
notes: Nonprofit; one of Indiana's only dedicated arthouses; hosts Hobnobben Film Festival. Eventive tenant (bucket + public api_key discovered from the tenant JS bundle at runtime). Screens show as "Main Theater" and "Spectator Lounge" in event data. Address/coords from research report — re-verify against their visit page.

name: Michigan Theater
notes: Historic 1928 movie palace (1,600+ seats main auditorium + screening room), operated by the Marquee Arts nonprofit. Sells via Agile Ticketing (org MT_3768). Also hosts concerts/live events (symphony, UMS, Sonic Lunch) — the adapter keeps only titles present in /all-films, dropping those. venue_match "Michigan Theater" also catches its "Foyer" events. Coords approx — verify.

name: State Theatre
notes: Restored 1942 Art Deco house (4 screens), Marquee Arts' second Ann Arbor venue. Same Agile backend and site as the Michigan; venue_match "State Theatre" claims only its screenings. Coords approx — verify.

name: Trylon Cinema
notes: Nonprofit repertory microcinema, on-site WordPress cart. Homepage "upcoming by date" widget server-renders ~29 days (date buttons 1:1 with day lists; no year on dates — adapter rolls the year forward). Titles carry "in 4K/35mm" suffixes and "*Sold Out*" markers. No film year on site — enrichment leans on the ambiguity guard + overrides. Coords approx — verify.

name: Hi-Pointe Theatre
notes: St. Louis' only nonprofit-owned movie theatre (operated by Cinema St. Louis). Historic 1922 single-screen plus micro "Backlot" screen. MOVED Eventive -> Filmbot (discovered 2026-07-06: the old hi-pointe.eventive.org tenant still exists but serves an empty schedule; hipointetheatre.org is "powered by Filmbot" with the standard WP REST route). The research report's platform info was stale — a good venue-updater test case. Coords approx — verify.

name: Film Streams at the Ruth Sokolof Theater
notes: Omaha's nonprofit cinema (est. 2007). Two buildings share one site and Blackbaud-hosted ticketing; venue_match claims only the Ruth Sokolof's screenings (screens Ruth & Mary). The site server-renders a per-day schedule at /films/date/YYYY-MM-DD with a data-time attribute on every showtime (no year-rollover guessing). Coords approx — verify.

name: Film Streams at the Dundee Theater
notes: Film Streams' second building (the historic 1925 Dundee, restored and reopened 2018): the 300-seat Peggy Payne Theater and the 25-seat Linder Microcinema. Same site/adapter as the Ruth Sokolof; venue_match claims only the Dundee's screenings. Coords approx — verify.

name: Gateway Film Center
notes: Nonprofit (Gateway Film Foundation, 501c3), Sundance satellite venue, adjacent to Ohio State. Filmbot WordPress plugin exposes /wp-json/nj/v1/showtime/listings — one JSON call, all upcoming showtimes. Series like "Cult 101" appear inside movie names. Coords approx — verify.

name: The Neon
notes: Dayton's premier arthouse (nonprofit). Filmbot's NEWER hosted product: React SPA + GraphQL at /graphql. The filmbot_graphql adapter works (verified 2026-07-06, tenant ids above), BUT their robots.txt explicitly disallows /graphql — so per our ground rules we DO NOT scrape it. Next step per the brief: email the venue and ask; flip adapter to filmbot_graphql if they say yes.

name: Cleveland Institute of Art Cinematheque
notes: "One of the country's best repertory movie theaters" (NYT); program of the Cleveland Institute of Art. www.cia.edu sits behind a 307 bot challenge, but the cinematheque lives on its own WordPress at cinematheque.cia.edu with the standard Filmbot REST route (theater_id 348) — same adapter as Gateway/Varsity/Hi-Pointe.

name: Oriental Theatre
notes: Milwaukee Film (nonprofit, est. 2008); historic 1927 movie palace, 35mm/70mm reinstated after renovation; hosts Milwaukee Film Festival. Shares an Eventive bucket with the Downer — venue_match claims only events whose Eventive venue name contains "oriental". Coords approx.

name: Downer Theatre
notes: Milwaukee Film's second historic cinema (1915, Milwaukee's oldest operating movie house). Same Eventive bucket as the Oriental; venue_match "downer". Coords approx.
